title: selenicereus undatus
intro: Selenicereus undatus, also known as the night-blooming cereus or queen of the night, is a species of flowering cactus native to the Americas, characterized by its long, curved stems and fragrant, white flowers that bloom only at night. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Mexico and Central America, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and combat fungal infections. The health benefits of Selenicereus undatus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress relief. Herbal preparations of Selenicereus undatus can be made from the plant's stems, roots, or flowers, and are often used in teas, tinctures, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
